title: आनन्दलहरी
devanagariText:
॥ श्री आनन्दलहरी ॥

शिवः शक्त्या युक्तो यदि भवति शक्तः प्रभवितुं
न चेदेवं देवो न खलु कुशलः स्पन्दितुमपि।
अतस्त्वामाराध्यां हरिहरविरिञ्चादिभिरपि
प्रणन्तुं स्तोतुं वा कथमकृतपुण्यः प्रभवति॥ १॥

तनीयांसं पांसुं तव चरणपङ्केरुहभवं
विरिञ्चिस्सन्धत्ते विरचयितुं लोकानविकलम्।
वहत्येनं शौरिः कथमपि सहस्रेण शिरसां
हरः संक्षुद्यैनं भजति भसितोद्धूलनविधिम्॥ २॥

अविद्यानामन्तस्तिमिरमिहिरद्वीपनगरी
जडानां चैतन्यस्तबकमकरन्दश्रुतिझरी।
दरिद्राणां चिन्तामणिगुणनिका जन्मजलधौ
nimagnānāṁ दंष्ट्रा मुररिपुवराहस्य भवति॥ ३॥

त्वदन्यः पाणिभ्यामभयवरदो दैवतगणः
त्वमेका नैवासि प्रकटितवराभीत्यभिनया।
भयात्त्रातुं दातुं फलमपि च वाञ्छासमधिकं
शरण्ये लोकानां तव हि चरणावेव निपुणौ॥ ४॥

हरिस्त्वामाराध्य प्रणतजनसौभाग्यजननीं
पुरा नारी भूत्वा पुररिपुमपि क्षोभमनयत्।
स्मरोऽपि त्वां नत्वा रतिनयनलेह्येन वपुषा
मुनीनामप्यन्तः प्रभवति हि मोहाय महताम्॥ ५॥

धनुः पौष्पं मौर्वी मधुकरमयी पञ्च विशिखाः
वसन्तः सामन्तो मलयमरुदायोधनरथः।
तथाप्येकः सर्वं हिमगिरिसुते कामपि कृपां
अपाङ्गात्ते लब्ध्वा जगदिदमनङ्गो विजयते॥ ६॥

क्वणत्काञ्चीदामा कुचभरनतस्ताचगलितं
कुचौ तुङ्गौ कुर्वन्ननिमिषकृपापूर्णनयनम्।
पदन्यासक्रीडापरिचयमिवाराभटमनः
स्वभावं ते वक्तुं स्फुरति हि मृदामातिशयिनी॥ ७॥

सुधासिन्धोर्मध्ये सुरविटपिवाटीपरिवृते
मणिद्वीपे नीपोपवनवति चिन्तामणिगृहे।
शिवाकारे मञ्चे परमशिवपर्यङ्कनिलयां
भजामि त्वां शश्वद्भजनपरमानन्दलहरीम्॥ ८॥

भवानि त्वं दासे मयि वितर दृष्टिं सकरुणां
इति स्तोतुं वाञ्छन्कथयति भवानि त्वमिति यः।
तदैव त्वं तस्मै दिशसि निजसायुज्यपदवीं
मुकुन्दब्रह्मेन्द्रस्फुटमकुटनीराजितपदाम्॥ ९॥

त्वयाऽऽहृत्य प्रच्छ्यं वषिणभुजगनायकशिवं
त्वया जिम्भन्मुक्तं प्रतिदिनमिदं त्रैतमुखरम्।
नमस्ते नित्याय त्रिगुणात्मने शक्त्यनुगते
प्रपञ्चं सिन्धुं वा वषति गिरिशे त्रिश्रवणतः॥ १०॥

॥ इति श्रीमच्छंकराचार्यविरचिता श्री आनन्दलहरी सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ananda Lahari ||

Shivah shaktya yukto yadi bhavati shaktah prabhavitum
Na ched-evam Devo na khalu kushalah spanditum-api |
Atas-tvam-aradhyam Hari-Hara-Virinchadi-bhir-api
Pranantum stotum va katham-akrita-punyah prabhavati || 1 ||

Taniyamsam paamsum tava charana-pankeruha-bhavam
Virinchis-sandhatte virachayitum lokan-avikalam |
Vahaty-enam Shaurih katham-api sahasrena shirasam
Harah samkshudya-enam bhajati bhasita-uddhulana-vidhim || 2 ||

Avidyanam-antas-timira-mihira-dvipa-nagari
Jadanam chaitanya-stabaka-makaranda-shruti-jhari |
Daridraanam chintamani-gunanika janma-jaladhau
Nimagnanam damshtra Mura-ripu-varahasya bhavati || 3 ||

Tvad-anyah panibhyam-abhaya-varado daivata-ganah
Tvam-eka na-eva-asi prakatita-vara-abhity-abhinaya |
Bhayat-tratum datum phalam-api cha vanchha-sam-adhikam
Sharanye lokanam tava hi charanav-eva nipunau || 4 ||

Haris-tvam-aradhya pranata-jana-saubhagya-jananim
Pura nari bhutva Pura-ripum-api kshobham-anayat |
Smaro-api tvam natva rati-nayana-lehyena vapusha
Muninam-apy-antah prabhavati hi mohaya mahatam || 5 ||

Dhanuh paushpam maurvi madhu-kara-mayi pancha vishikhah
Vasantah samanto Malaya-marud-ayodhana-rathah |
Tathaapy-ekah sarvam Hima-giri-sute kam-api kripam
Apangat-te labdhva jagad-idam-Anango vijayate || 6 ||

Kvanat-kanchi-dama kucha-bhara-natas-tacha-galitam
Kuchau tungau kurvan-animisha-kripa-purna-nayanam |
Pada-nyasa-krida-parichayam-iva-arabhatam-anah
Svabhavam te vaktum sphurati hi mridam-atishayini || 7 ||

Sudha-sindhor-madhye sura-vitapi-vati-parivrite
Mani-dvipe nipa-upavana-vati Chintamani-grihe |
Shiva-akare manche Parama-Shiva-paryanka-nilayam
Bhajami tvam shashvad-bhajana-parama-ananda-laharim || 8 ||

Bhavani tvam dase mayi vitara drishtim sa-karunam
Iti stotum vaanchhan kathayati Bhavani tvam-iti yah |
Tadaiva tvam tasmai dishasi nija-sayujya-padavim
Mukunda-Brahma-Indra-sphuta-makuta-nirajita-padam || 9 ||

Tvaya-ahritya prachchhyam vashin-abhujaga-nayaka-Shivam
Tvaya jimbhan-muktam pratidinам-idam traitam-ukharam |
Namaste nityaya tri-guna-atmane shakty-anugate
Prapancham sindhum va vashati Girishe tri-shravanatah || 10 ||

|| Iti Shrimach-Shankaracharya-virachita Shri Ananda Lahari Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
शिव शक्ति से युक्त होकर ही सृष्टि करने में समर्थ होते हैं; अन्यथा देव स्पन्दन भी नहीं कर सकते। अतः हरि-हर-ब्रह्मा आदि द्वारा भी आराध्य — पुण्यहीन कैसे प्रणाम या स्तुति कर सकता है?॥ १॥

आपके चरण-कमल की रज लेकर ब्रह्मा लोकों की रचना करते हैं, विष्णु सहस्र शिरों से उसे वहन करते हैं, शिव उसे पीसकर भस्म-उद्धूलन करते हैं॥ २॥

अविद्या के अन्धकार में सूर्यद्वीप-नगरी, जड़ों में चैतन्य-स्तबक, दरिद्रों के लिए चिन्तामणि, जन्मसागर में डूबतों के लिए वराहदंष्ट्रा — यही आपकी कृपा है॥ ३॥

अन्य देवता हाथों से अभय और वर देते हैं, पर आप तो ऐसा इशारा भी नहीं करतीं — भय से रक्षा और इच्छा से अधिक फल देने में आपके चरण ही निपुण हैं॥ ४॥

हरि ने आपकी आराध्या (सौभाग्यजननी) को पूजकर स्त्रीरूप धारण कर शिव को क्षुब्ध किया। कामदेव ने आपको नमन कर अपने रूप से महामुनियों को भी मोहित किया॥ ५॥

फूलों का धनुष, भ्रमरों की प्रत्यञ्चा, पाँच बाण, वसन्त सामन्त, मलय पवन रथ — फिर भी अकेला अनंग, हे हिमगिरिसुते, आपकी कृपाकटाक्ष पाकर सम्पूर्ण जगत को जीत लेता है॥ ६॥

सुधासिन्धु के मध्य मणिद्वीप में चिन्तामणिगृह में शिवाकार मंच पर परमशिव-पर्यंक पर आसीन — उस शाश्वत भजनपरमानन्दलहरी को मैं भजता हूँ॥ ८॥

'भवानी, इस दास पर करुणादृष्टि करो' — ऐसा कहने की इच्छा से जो 'भवानि त्वम्' इतना ही कहता है, उसे तुरन्त आप अपनी सायुज्यपदवी प्रदान करती हैं — जिस पद को मुकुन्द, ब्रह्मा, इन्द्र अपने मुकुटों से नीराजन करते हैं॥ ९॥
benefits: First part of Saundarya Lahari — wave of bliss by Shankaracharya
Supreme Shakti stotram revealing Devi as the power behind all gods
Invokes saubhagya, beauty, and fulfillment beyond desire
Merely saying 'Bhavani tvam' grants sayujya with the Goddess
Describes Devi's abode in Mani Dwipa — the jeweled island
Ideal for Navaratri, Fridays, and Lalita Jayanti worship
description: The Ananda Lahari, known in Sanskrit as आनन्दलहरी, is a sacred prayer in praise of Goddess Parvati. Attributed to Ananda Lahari (Saundarya Lahari Part 1) — Adi Shankaracharya, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to grant saubhagya, beauty, and fulfillment beyond desire and describe Devi's abode in Mani Dwipa — the jeweled island. It is also said to supreme Shakti stotram revealing Devi as the power behind all gods. This sacred text is ideally recited on Friday (Shukravar) and Tuesday (Mangalvar) and during Navratri and Lalita Panchami for enhanced blessings. The text contains 10 verses and takes around 8 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Ananda Lahari in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ananda Lahari?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ananda Lahari (श्री आनन्दलहरी — 'the Wave of Bliss') is the celebrated first part of the Soundarya Lahari (the larger 'Wave of Beauty'), comprising verses 1-41 of the 100-verse opus traditionally attributed to Adi Shankaracharya (788-820 CE), the founder of the Advaita Vedanta tradition. The Ananda Lahari is uniquely revered as the more esoteric, Tantric and mantric portion — every verse is said to be a Yantra and Mantra unto itself, with corresponding bija-mantras and yantra diagrams preserved in the Sri Vidya tradition for the worship of Goddess Lalita Tripura Sundari / Rajarajeshvari. The text opens with the cosmic declaration Shivah Shaktya yukto yadi bhavati shaktah prabhavitum, na ched-evam Devo na khalu kushalah spanditum-api — 'Shiva united with Shakti is able to create; otherwise the Lord cannot even stir' — establishing the foundational Shakta theology of the Sri Vidya tradition. The Ananda Lahari is one of the supreme stotras of the Tripura Sundari upasana. (Source: Adi Shankaracharya tradition; Sri Vidya corpus.)

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Ananda Lahari and how is it related to Soundarya Lahari?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ananda Lahari (verses 1-41) and Soundarya Lahari (verses 42-100) together form a single 100-verse opus traditionally attributed to Adi Shankaracharya. According to Sri Vidya tradition, the Ananda Lahari was originally inscribed by Lord Shiva himself on the wall of Mount Kailasa; Shankaracharya is said to have ascended Kailasa, memorized these verses, and brought them down to the world. As he descended, Nandi (Shiva's bull) tore away half the manuscript — only the first 41 verses (Ananda Lahari) survived. Shankara then composed the remaining 59 verses (Soundarya Lahari proper) himself to complete the 100. This explains why the Ananda Lahari portion is considered more dense, more Tantric and more directly emanative of Shiva's own teaching — every verse encoding both a yantra and a bija mantra — while the Soundarya Lahari portion is more lyrical and devotional. The complete 100-verse opus is the supreme stotra of the Sri Vidya / Tripura Sundari tradition, alongside the Lalita Sahasranama and Trishati. (Source: Sri Vidya tradition; Adi Shankara hagiography.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the famous opening verse of the Ananda Lahari?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ananda Lahari opens with one of the most quoted verses in all of Hindu philosophy: Shivah shaktya yukto yadi bhavati shaktah prabhavitum, na ched-evam Devo na khalu kushalah spanditum-api, atas-tvam-aradhyam Hari-Hara-Virinchadi-bhir-api, pranantum stotum va katham-akrita-punyah prabhavati. The translation: 'Lord Shiva united with Shakti becomes capable of creation; without her, the Lord cannot even stir; therefore O Devi, who is worshipped even by Hari, Hara and Brahma — how can one without accumulated merit dare to bow to or praise you?' This verse establishes the foundational Shakta theology that Shiva is the static substratum (akarta) and Shakti is the dynamic cosmic principle (kartri) — Shiva alone cannot create, sustain or destroy; only Shiva-Shakti united (yamala) can manifest the universe. This is the Sri Vidya theological foundation: Shakti is the active principle without whom even the supreme Brahman is inert. The verse is also celebrated as one of the most beautiful pieces of Sanskrit philosophical poetry. (Source: Ananda Lahari verse 1.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Bhavani Tvam verse and why is it famous?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 22 of the Soundarya Lahari (which appears in some recensions in the Ananda Lahari portion as verse 9) is the celebrated Bhavani Tvam verse: Bhavani tvam dase mayi vitara drishtim sa-karunam, iti stotum vanchhan kathayati Bhavani tvam-iti yah, tadaiva tvam tasmai dishasi nija-sayujya-padavim, Mukunda-Brahma-Indra-sphuta-makuta-nirajita-padam. The translation: 'O Bhavani, on me your servant cast your compassionate glance — wishing to praise you thus, the moment one merely says Bhavani tvam (O Bhavani, you), at that very moment you bestow upon him your own sayujya-padavi (state of complete union with you), the lotus-feet at which the bright crowns of Mukunda (Vishnu), Brahma and Indra are arati-waved'. The verse celebrates the ananta karuna (boundless compassion) of Devi — even before the devotee can complete his prayer, the mere syllables 'Bhavani tvam' instantly grant moksha-sayujya. This is the supreme bhakti teaching of the Ananda Lahari: Devi's grace is so spontaneous that grammar itself becomes liberation — Bhavani tvam ('O Bhavani, you') and Bhavani tvam ('I become you') are the same words. (Source: Ananda Lahari verse 9 / Soundarya Lahari verse 22.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Ananda Lahari be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ananda Lahari is most powerful on Friday (Shukravar — sacred to Shukra and Lalita Tripura Sundari), on Tuesday (Mangalvar — sacred to Devi), during all nine days of Sharad Navratri and Chaitra Navratri (especially Maha Ashtami / Durga Ashtami), on Lalita Panchami (Ashvin Shukla Panchami — sacred to Lalita Tripura Sundari), on Lalita Jayanti (Magha Purnima), on every Pournami (full moon — sacred to Devi as Chandra-shekhari), on Shankara Jayanti (Vaishakha Shukla Panchami — birthday of Adi Shankaracharya), on Guru Purnima, and during Sri Vidya upasana. The traditional recitation is at dawn (Brahma-muhurta) after a bath, facing east, before a Sri Yantra (the supreme yantra of Tripura Sundari) or a Devi murti, with kumkum, red hibiscus, akshata, fruits, kheer naivedya, and a ghee diya. Each verse may be recited with its specific bija and yantra meditation as preserved in the Sri Vidya tradition. The full Ananda Lahari (41 verses) takes about 30 minutes; selected verses (especially 1, 8, 9, 22) may be recited daily. (Sources: Sri Vidya upasana tradition; Adi Shankara stotra paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Ananda Lahari grant according to tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ananda Lahari is considered one of the most powerful Devi stotras for: (1) Saubhagya (auspicious married life, especially for women) — including the boons of beauty, health, charm and harmonious relationships, (2) Sri (wealth, prosperity, abundance) — Devi as Mahalakshmi-Tripura Sundari grants both material and spiritual riches, (3) Vidya (knowledge, learning, wisdom) — Devi as Sarasvati-Tripura Sundari grants both laukika (worldly) and apara (esoteric) vidya, (4) Santati (children) — especially for couples seeking offspring, (5) Aishvarya (royal sovereignty, leadership, authority), (6) Yoga-siddhi — the awakening of Kundalini Shakti in the muladhara and her ascent through the six chakras to sahasrara (this is the inner meaning of Soundarya Lahari verses 9-10), (7) Mukti — sayujya with Devi (verse 9 / SL 22), (8) Specific bija-yantra-siddhis for each of the 41 verses as preserved in Sri Vidya tradition (verse 1 for getting healthy children, verse 8 for vision of Devi's Mani Dvipa, verse 22 for instant moksha, etc.). Each verse is a complete Tantric sadhana. (Source: Ananda Lahari Sri Vidya tradition.)
